title: RevOps Manager
description: Hands-on RevOps role configuring and administering enterprise CRM systems like Salesforce, including custom object/field creation, lead/opportunity management, territory structuring, and inbound lead routing. Responsible for building and managing integrations across the tech stack (CRM, marketing automation, customer success platforms) for clean data flows and accurate hand-offs between Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success. Owns data governance, revenue KPI tracking, forecasting models, and drives continuous improvement projects piloting emerging RevOps technologies. Requires fluency with RevOps tools like LeanData, Demandbase, and Catalyst in a high-growth software environment.
score_reason: Exceptional match for a senior RevOps executive—the role aligns perfectly with Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS GTM, core competencies in Salesforce administration, pipeline forecasting, data governance, and RevOps tool stack management. Remote location is ideal, and the hands-on CRM configuration work combined with strategic KPI ownership matches his sweet spot.
ai_opener: Omri here—your RevOps Manager role caught my attention because fal's approach to enterprise CRM administration and cross-functional data governance mirrors the revenue infrastructure challenges I've spent 15 years solving in high-growth B2B SaaS. I've built similar Salesforce architectures, implemented lead routing logic with tools like LeanData, and owned the KPI frameworks that drive forecasting accuracy—I'd love to explore how I could accelerate your revenue operations maturity.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Serves as a key partner for Sales and RevOps, providing dashboards, reporting tools, and regional insights to optimize territory performance. Redesigns territory models and processes for global scalability and long-term operational durability. Partners with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory compositions through quantitative and qualitative analyses, aligning territories with business goals and headcount plans. Supports GTM planning cycles by delivering territory assignments, whitespace analysis, and coverage recommendations. Maintains accurate territory mapping in systems like Fullcast and Salesforce, ensuring data integrity and change management.
score_reason: Strong alignment with RevOps core competencies (Salesforce, territory optimization, pipeline forecasting, cross-functional GTM planning) at a established SaaS company. Primary gap: role is more specialized in territory strategy than broader RevOps leadership, slightly below target seniority for 15+ year executive.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed Gong is building out Territory Strategy & Operations—a function that sits at the intersection of RevOps rigor and GTM strategy. With your background scaling B2B SaaS GTM operations and pipeline forecasting across multiple regions, you'd likely see immediate opportunities to enhance both the quantitative territory modeling and the cross-functional sales/finance alignment this role demands.

title: Revenue Operations Manager - Systems
location: New York / San Francisco / Salt Lake City (Hybrid) or Remote
description: Builds and owns the Salesforce environment for a new business division—objects, fields, validation rules, flows, and automations. Designs revenue tech stack integrations (CRM, enrichment, routing, marketing) and establishes system architecture standards and governance that scale. Architects the sales process from lead-to-close including forecasting methodology and deal hygiene. Requires 5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or GTM Operations with at least 2 years in a senior capacity, deep Salesforce configuration expertise, and familiarity with Outreach, LeanData, Clay, and HubSpot. Builder mentality essential.
score_reason: Exceptional match—15+ years B2B SaaS experience far exceeds 5+ year requirement, deep Salesforce expertise aligns perfectly with core responsibilities, and RevOps/GTM background directly covers tech stack integration, forecasting, and system architecture needs. Remote eligibility and builder mentality are ideal fits.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Flex's Revenue Operations Manager role and immediately thought of your background—architecting Salesforce environments and revenue tech stacks from the ground up is exactly what you've been scaling for senior teams. With your 15+ years building GTM infrastructure and deep hands-on expertise with Salesforce, enrichment tools, and forecasting methodologies, you'd be uniquely positioned to establish system governance standards for a new business division. I'd love to explore how your experience could accelerate Flex's revenue operations foundation.

title: Full-Stack GTM Engineer
description: Join an AI legal services startup building the autopilot for legal work. The company believes that within five years, more than 80% of legal work will pass through a model before reaching a human, helping law firms restructure their delivery model around AI. This Full-Stack GTM Engineer role focuses on building tools and automations that power the go-to-market motion. Ideal for builder-minded engineers excited about transforming how legal services get bought and procured at a high-growth, mission-driven company.
score_reason: Strong GTM engineering focus and AI-native positioning align well with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise. However, the legal tech vertical is outside his core B2B SaaS/Fintech experience, and the role emphasizes engineering/builder skills over revenue operations leadership.
ai_opener: August's vision of AI transforming legal services procurement resonates with modern GTM thinking. Your full-stack GTM engineering approach—building automations that power go-to-market motion—is exactly what scaling B2B companies need. Omri's 15+ years optimizing revenue operations and pipeline forecasting in high-growth SaaS could accelerate how you scale customer acquisition and operationalize your AI-first GTM model.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer and B2B research, used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange. As the company scales rapidly, this GTM Engineer will code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations that make the GTM team highly effective. Responsibilities include building lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, faster onboarding flows, and AI-powered workflows and agents that eliminate hours of manual work each week. Requires strong engineering skills and a builder mindset.
score_reason: While the GTM Engineer title and remote location align with Omri's profile, this role heavily emphasizes coding/engineering skills and building technical tools, which is a weak match for a RevOps executive focused on strategy, process, and systems integration rather than software development.
ai_opener: I noticed Conveo is scaling fast and building AI-native research tools for major brands—impressive customer roster. As someone who's spent 15+ years optimizing GTM operations and pipeline forecasting in high-growth B2B SaaS, I'm curious if you're also looking to optimize your internal GTM workflows and RevOps stack as you scale, beyond the engineering tooling layer.

title: Staff GTM Engineer
description: 1Password builds the foundation for a safe, productive digital future, with a mission to unleash employee productivity without compromising security. The company pioneered Extended Access Management, a cybersecurity category built for how people and AI agents work today, and is trusted by over 165,000 businesses. This Staff GTM Engineer role involves building the technical systems and automations that power the go-to-market motion at one of the most loved brands in cybersecurity. Ideal for senior engineers who enjoy solving hard problems in a fast-paced environment.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, remote eligibility, and B2B SaaS/cybersecurity domain expertise. However, 'Staff' level suggests IC/engineering-heavy role vs. Omri's RevOps leadership background, and cybersecurity is outside core fintech/AI-native GTM sweet spot.
ai_opener: 1Password's leadership in Extended Access Management caught my attention—especially how you're building GTM motion for an AI-native world. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue systems, I've built the exact technical infrastructure (pipeline forecasting, CAC/LTV analytics, sales automation) that powers modern GTM at scale. I'd be curious if you're looking for someone who can bridge RevOps strategy with the engineering rigor this role demands.

title: Head of Demand Generation & Growth
description: Growth leadership role owning pipeline and revenue targets through digital channels. Requires 7+ years of progressive B2B SaaS marketing experience including at least 3 years in a growth leadership role, with deep expertise in paid acquisition, SEO, website optimization, and full-funnel analytics. Experience with enterprise buyer journeys and ABM motions essential. Proficiency with tools like Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, GA4, HubSpot, Salesforce, Clay, n8n, SEMrush, and Looker or Tableau required. A collaborative, data-driven leader with experience managing internal teams and agencies.
